entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop studied for productivity and mechanical harvesting suitability

entities.name: SUGARCANE
entities.description: A crop used for inter-cropping with chickpea cultivar HC 5

entities.name: HC 5
entities.description: A tall and erect chickpea cultivar bred for inter-cropping and mechanical harvesting

entities.name: JAKI 9218
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar used for comparison in planting density studies

entities.name: PLANT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The physical structure and morphology of the plant, including height and erectness

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output or yield of the crop

entities.name: MECHANICAL HARVESTING
entities.description: The process of harvesting crops using machinery

entities.name: PLANTING DENSITY
entities.description: The number of plants per unit area, determined by plant spacing

entities.name: PLANT SPACING
entities.description: The distance between plants and rows used to control planting density

entities.name: CROP GROWTH
entities.description: The developmental progress and physical growth of the plant

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: PRIMARY BRANCH
entities.description: The main branches emerging from the plant stem

entities.name: SECONDARY BRANCH
entities.description: The smaller branches emerging from the primary branches

entities.name: PODS PER PLANT
entities.description: The number of pods produced by a single plant

entities.name: INTRA-ROW SPACING
entities.description: The distance between plants within the same row

entities.name: INTER-ROW SPACING
entities.description: The distance between different rows of plants

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant, related to erectness

entities.name: GROUND CLEARANCE
entities.description: The height of the first pod from the ground level

entities.name: INTER-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously in the same field
